pattern: Reduce the bracket to a memoized recursion over (current player count, the two target players' 1-indexed positions), counting how many between-players can survive a round to derive the targets' next positions.
hints: Only the two special players' positions matter; everyone else just shifts the targets around, so memoize on the count of players plus the two positions.
Two front-positioned players p and q meet exactly when q is the mirror of p (their indices sum to count+1); otherwise both win and you pick outcomes for the rest.

Use the symmetry that lets you assume the first target is at least as close to the front as the second is to the back, then enumerate winners before the first target and how many players between the two survive, mapping to the next round's positions.

edge_inputs.description: they meet immediately at the ends
mirror positions in a larger field

stress.description: random valid n and two distinct players
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, 28)
    a = rng.randint(1, n)
    b = rng.randint(1, n)
    while b == a:
        b = rng.randint(1, n)
    f, s = min(a, b), max(a, b)
    return [n, f, s]
